content: src/ui/ is an isolated sub-package: own package.json, tsconfig (with project references for app+node), vite.config.ts, deps. Root tsconfig.json excludes src/ui; root biome.json excludes src/ui (not just src/ui/dist). The boundary between server and UI is the HTTP wire, so the UI duplicates row types in src/ui/src/api/types.ts rather than importing src/db/schema.ts.

title: HashRouter not BrowserRouter for the warren UI
rationale: API routes /runs/:id, /agents/:name are registered on the same Bun.serve and would shadow SPA deep links on a hard reload (e.g., GET /runs/abc123 → JSON, not index.html). HashRouter uses /#/runs/abc123 which lives entirely on the client; the server only ever sees / and serves index.html. Considered routing API under /api/ but SPEC §8.1 fixes the surface as flat. Considered Accept-header content negotiation but it's brittle. HashRouter is the cheapest V1 fix.

description: Vite + React tsc -b build inside src/ui/ fails with duplicate type declarations from bun-types in parent node_modules. tsc's auto type discovery walks up to the warren root node_modules and pulls in bun-types whose globals (ImportMeta.hot, ImportMetaEnv, Worker) conflict with vite/client and DOM lib.
resolution: Set 'types': [] in src/ui/tsconfig.app.json (rely on /// <reference types='vite/client' /> in src/vite-env.d.ts for client types). Set 'types': ['node'] in tsconfig.node.json for vite.config.ts (Node API). Add @types/node to devDeps. Set skipLibCheck: true in both as a belt-and-suspenders for any other stray @types parent.

content: UI auth: bearer token (WARREN_API_TOKEN) lives in localStorage under key 'warren.apiToken'. API client adds 'Authorization: Bearer <token>' to every fetch + NDJSON stream; on 401 it clears the cached token and throws UnauthorizedError so AuthGate redirects to /login. Login page probes via /agents (always 200 for valid token) rather than /readyz (returns 503 when zero agents are registered, which would falsely reject a fresh install).

name: NDJSON event stream consumption
description: useEventStream(runId, follow) reads the streamRunEvents async iterator (chunked NDJSON over fetch). follow=true keeps the connection open and reconnects on transport error with exponential backoff capped at 30s; lastSeqRef carries the highest seen seq so reconnects send ?since=lastSeq+1 to skip events already in the buffer. follow=false replays history once and stops (used for terminal runs). UnauthorizedError aborts permanently; the AuthGate redirects on the next render.

name: flex-main-min-w-0
description: Layout's <main className='flex-1'> wrapper must include min-w-0 so flex items shrink below their content's intrinsic min-width. Without it, wide tables (Projects, Runs) push the pane past 100vw and the page scrolls horizontally — even though the Table primitive already wraps in a 'w-full overflow-auto' div. min-w-0 unlocks the inner overflow-auto so tables scroll within their card instead of expanding the layout. Same trap for any future flex container holding scrollable content.

name: event-driven-runs-invalidation
description: RunDetail watches stream.events for kinds in REFETCH_TRIGGER_KINDS={state_change, cancel.requested, reap.completed, reap_failed} and calls qc.invalidateQueries({queryKey: ['runs']}) (no exact, so both ['runs', id] and the list page's ['runs', filter] refresh). Eliminates the stale-badge-after-cancel UX where the event arrived in the stream but the GET /runs/:id wasn't re-issued. The 3-5s refetchInterval polling stays as a backstop.

content: Mutation feedback in RunDetail uses the same inline text-xs pattern as SteerForm (no toast library is wired). CancelStatus reads mutation.isError/isSuccess/data.{alreadyTerminal,burrowRun.state} and renders feedback below the button. Avoids pulling sonner/react-hot-toast for one button.

RunDetail's empty-push and +N-commits header badges (warren-f3bb) read the latest reap.completed event off stream.events — the runs row itself doesn't store commitsAhead. Use extractReapSummary(events) to walk events backward, find kind==='reap.completed', narrow the unknown payload to ReapCompletedPayload, and read branchPushed + commitsAhead. Both badges only fire when branchPushed===true: 'empty push' (destructive variant) when commitsAhead===0; '+N commit(s)' (succeeded variant) when commitsAhead>0; nothing when commitsAhead is null (couldn't compute).

RunDetail surfaces reap-opened PR (warren-f6af) two ways: a 'PR ↗' header link in the badge row next to the +N-commits badge, and a dedicated 'Pull Request' MetaCard with the full URL when r.prUrl is non-null. Both read directly from RunRow.prUrl (mirrored from runs.pr_url), not from the reap.completed event payload — the run query is invalidated on reap.completed via REFETCH_TRIGGER_KINDS so the header refreshes without polling.

NewRunPage exposes a free-text 'Branch / tag / SHA (optional)' Input between the Project select and Prompt textarea (warren-7589). Selected project's defaultBranch is the placeholder. Empty/whitespace input is omitted from the POST /runs body via spread (...(trimmedRef.length > 0 ? { ref: trimmedRef } : {})) so the server's optionalString doesn't see an empty string and refreshProject falls back to project.defaultBranch. No remote-branch enumeration — the seed deferred git ls-remote on form load.

src/ui/src/api/client.ts runsApi.create takes a CreateRunInput type (defined in api/types.ts) so callers can spread an optional ref alongside agent/project/prompt without inlining the shape at every call site (warren-7589).

Project detail page lives at hash route /#/projects/:id (src/ui/src/pages/ProjectDetail.tsx). Wired in App.tsx after the /projects list route; the projects table makes the ID column a Link so the list is the entry point.

title: ProjectDetail reuses the projects-list cache
rationale: There is no GET /projects/:id endpoint — the list endpoint is the only project-row source. ProjectDetail useQuery(['projects']).data?.projects.find(p => p.id === id) avoids a redundant HTTP route and shares the cache with the list page so navigation back-and-forth never refetches the row.

content: Warren config panel (ProjectDetail.tsx) renders three blocks per LoadedWarrenConfig wire envelope: TriggersBlock (cron entries as cards), DefaultsBlock (defaultRole/defaultBranch/defaultPrompt as dl), ErrorsBlock (per-file failures, hidden when errors.length === 0). null fields render an EmptyHint instead of being skipped — operators see what's absent.

src/ui/src/api/types.ts mirrors the warren-config wire envelope manually (WarrenConfigResponse, Trigger, CronTrigger, DefaultsConfig, WarrenConfigFileError, WarrenConfigFileErrorCode). Kept in sync by hand because src/ui/ is excluded from the root tsconfig (mx-1bd551) — the boundary is the HTTP wire, not a TS import.

NewRun (src/ui/src/pages/NewRun.tsx) auto-fills its agent picker from the selected project's .warren/defaults.json defaultRole (warren-fd14): a useEffect watches projectsApi.warrenConfig and sets agent=defaultRole when the role matches a registered agent and the user hasn't touched the picker. The 'agentTouched' state latches on the first user-driven onChange and never clears, so manual selections survive subsequent project switches. An inline text-xs hint under the select shows 'Defaulted from this project's .warren/defaults.json' when auto-filled, or a destructive-color warning when defaultRole exists but isn't a registered agent.

NewRun (src/ui/src/pages/NewRun.tsx) auto-fills its prompt textarea from the selected project's .warren/defaults.json defaultPrompt (warren-af38), mirroring the defaultRole agent-picker pattern (warren-fd14): a promptTouched flag latches off the first user keystroke so manual edits survive subsequent project switches; the inline 'Defaulted from this project's .warren/defaults.json' hint surfaces below the textarea while the value still equals the default. Both effects share the same warrenConfig query (queryKey ['projects', project, 'warren-config']).

TriggersBlock (ProjectDetail.tsx) consumes GET /projects/:id/triggers — TriggerSummary[] envelope adds lastFiredAt/nextFireAt/lastRunId/parseError on top of the parsed YAML fields and lives at its own react-query key ['projects', id, 'triggers'] separate from the warren-config query (which still feeds DefaultsBlock + ErrorsBlock). Run Now uses a single useMutation at block level keyed by mutation.variables===triggerId; onSuccess invalidates the triggers query + runs and navigates to /runs/:newRunId (mirrors NewRun's onSuccess pattern, mx-d87018).

src/ui/src/api/types.ts mirrors the /projects/:id/triggers wire envelope as TriggerSummary + TriggersResponse + RunTriggerResponse — kept in lockstep with src/triggers/summary.ts TriggerSummary (root tsconfig excludes src/ui, so the boundary is the HTTP wire). RunTriggerResponse reuses the existing RunRow + BurrowSummary types since POST /projects/:id/triggers/:triggerId/run returns the same envelope as POST /runs.

Agents page (src/ui/src/pages/Agents.tsx) header + empty-state copy frames built-in claude-code + sapling as the default, and the canopy library as opt-in custom-agent layer on top (set CANOPY_REPO_URL). Avoid copy like 'Canopy prompts tagged agent:true' or 'clone your canopy library' in the empty state — it implies users must adopt canopy to use warren. NewRun page (src/ui/src/pages/NewRun.tsx) subtitle says 'Spawn an agent against a project repo inside a fresh sandbox' (not 'a canopy agent ... burrow sandbox') for the same reason (warren-e576).

NewRun.tsx surfaces Provider override + Model override as paired free-text Inputs in a 2-col grid above the Prompt textarea (warren-f8c0). Auto-fill mirrors the agent picker pattern: read agent.renderedJson.frontmatter.{provider,model} via the local readFrontmatter() narrowing helper; populate the input until the operator types (providerTouched/modelTouched flags); trim empties before spawn.mutate so the wire stays optional-clean.

Cost display in RunDetail uses formatCostUsd(): >=1 USD → 2 decimals ('1.45'), <1 USD → 3 decimals ('0.045') so sub-cent cache-only turns don't round to zero. Rendered as a header badge (variant=succeeded, font-mono text-xs) with a tooltip listing tokens via formatTokens (>=1M -> '1.2M', >=1k -> '1.2k'). Runs.tsx list view exposes an opt-in Cost column persisted in localStorage under 'warren.runsList.showCostColumn'.

src/ui/src/pages/RunDetail.tsx EventLine (warren-70af) detects pi-runtime sub-kinds (compaction_start/end, auto_retry_start/end, extension_error, queue_update) by peeking at payload.type when event.kind is 'state_change' or 'telemetry' — burrow's pi parser (src/runtime/parsers/pi.ts) collapses pi's wide event vocabulary into burrow's stable taxonomy and stores the original pi envelope type in payload.type. PI_SUBKIND_LABELS maps each to a friendlier display label; extension_error additionally tints rose-700 like stderr. Kind-direct match (event.kind === 'compaction_start' etc.) is also honored so a future burrow release that promotes any of these to first-class kinds Just Works without a UI rev. Unknown kinds fall through to generic rendering (mx-0db923).

NewRun.tsx provider/model auto-fill follows the same precedence the server applies (warren-618b): project default (.warren/defaults.json.defaultProvider / defaultModel) > agent frontmatter. providerFromProjectDefault / modelFromProjectDefault drive the 'Defaulted from this project's .warren/defaults.json' label; falling back to 'Defaulted from agent frontmatter' when no project default is set. Auto-fill stops as soon as the operator touches the field.

Agents page (src/ui/src/pages/Agents.tsx, warren-f755) expanded-row renders a structured AgentDefinitionPanel — metadata grid (name/version/source/provider/model/tags), resolvedFrom breadcrumb, each sections.* as a native <details> (system open by default), and a 'View raw JSON' toggle for parity. The expanded TableCell uses 'max-w-0' plus 'whitespace-pre-wrap break-words' inside every <pre> so long unbroken section bodies wrap at the cell width instead of widening the table and triggering page-level horizontal scroll.

AgentRow (src/ui/src/api/types.ts) carries an optional 'source: "builtin" | "library"' field. The server decorates it in withAgentSource (src/server/handlers.ts:181) via readAgentSource(renderedJson). UI consumers (e.g. AgentDefinitionPanel) display it but treat it as optional since older deployments may not emit it.

EventLine (src/ui/src/pages/RunDetail.tsx, warren-3ad4) renders each RunEvent as a single-line <details> summary with seq/HH:MM:SS/displayKind/stream + a derived one-line summary; click expands to a pretty-printed JSON <pre>. summarizeEvent() defensively probes payload shape per kind: state_change reads payload.type then walks message.content for tool_use names and message.usage.{input,output,cost.total} to emit 'type · message (toolCall: X) · usage 154/23.8k · $0.026'; reap.* uses ReapCompletedPayload (state/branchPushed/commitsAhead/prUrl/errors); toolcall_*/tool_execution_* reads name/exit_code; text reads payload.text or delta; unknown shapes fall through to {text|message|reason|type} then JSON.stringify. group-open:hidden on the summary text drops the truncated preview once expanded. flex+min-w-0+truncate handles one-line clipping in the events scrollbox.

Cost column on /runs (src/ui/src/pages/Runs.tsx, warren-a7ec) is on by default. Default uses showCost=true; only an explicit '0' in localStorage key warren.runsList.showCostColumn hides the column. Rationale: pi runs are the common runtime and hydrate costUsd via in-stream extraction (warren-17a4).

RunDetail (src/ui/src/pages/RunDetail.tsx, warren-a7ec) renders a dedicated CostCard inside the meta grid that shows formatCostUsd(costUsd) as font-mono text-lg with a tokens-in/out/cache-r/cache-w breakdown via formatTokenBreakdown(run) inline (not hover-only). Null costUsd renders an em-dash; null token breakdown is omitted. The old header cost Badge is removed.

src/ui/src/pages/RunDetail.tsx PreviewCard (warren-c0b9) renders when run.previewState !== null: status badge mapped to badge variants (starting→queued amber, live→succeeded green, failed→failed rose, torn-down→cancelled gray), an 'Open ↗' link routed through buildPreviewLoginUrl (auth-exempt handshake — token in query, server 302s to run-<id>.<host>), and a 'Tear down' button (POST /runs/:id/preview/teardown with actor='ui'). Failure tail surfaces in a code block when state==='failed'.

src/ui/src/api/client.ts: buildPreviewLoginUrl(runId) reads the bearer from localStorage and builds /runs/<id>/preview/login?token=<bearer>. Used by RunDetail's PreviewCard to render an 'Open ↗' link — the server's resolvePreviewRedirect falls back to https://run-<id>.<previewHost>/ when ?redirect is omitted, so the UI never needs WARREN_PREVIEW_HOST locally. Returns null when no token cached.

src/ui/src/pages/RunDetail.tsx REFETCH_TRIGGER_KINDS includes preview_launched, preview_evicted, preview_torn_down (warren-c0b9). Each flips one of preview_state/preview_port/preview_started_at on the run row, so observing the event in the live NDJSON stream invalidates the run query and refreshes the PreviewCard without waiting for the 3s/5s polling backstop.

title: Preview teardown button only shown when previewState is in PREVIEW_ACTIVE_STATES ('starting'|'live')
rationale: Tearing down a 'failed' row would 200 with status='already-failed' and confuse the operator (nothing changed). Hiding the button for 'failed' and 'torn-down' makes the affordance only appear when it actually does something. The teardown route remains callable via API for scripting.

content: src/ui/src/pages/RunDetail.tsx PreviewCard (warren-016d) calls previewApi.config() with staleTime/gcTime=Infinity. Deployment-wide config (mode + host) only changes via a warren restart, so per-page refetch would be wasted cycles.

PreviewCard (src/ui/src/pages/RunDetail.tsx) renders a mode-aware canonical URL line via formatPreviewUrl(): path mode uses window.location.origin (or config.host when set) + /p/<id>/; subdomain mode uses https://run-<id>.<host>/. Mirrors server-side src/preview/launch.ts formatPreviewUrl().

Tailwind v4 @theme tokens cannot be flipped by an explicit attribute selector when wrapped in @media — to support both OS-following (prefers-color-scheme) and explicit user override (data-theme on <html>), keep light tokens in @theme and define dark tokens in two parallel raw-CSS selectors on :root: ':root[data-theme="dark"]' for the explicit case and '@media (prefers-color-scheme: dark) :root:not([data-theme])' for the OS-default case. Same --color-* oklch values in both blocks; @theme block unmodified. See src/ui/src/index.css (warren-d340).

Theme override persistence (warren-d340) lives at localStorage key 'warren.theme' with values 'light'|'dark'|'system'; 'system' is encoded as absence of the key. A FOUC-guard <script> in src/ui/index.html reads it synchronously and sets document.documentElement.dataset.theme before React paints — invalid stored values are dropped silently. useTheme (src/ui/src/hooks/useTheme.ts) is the single writer of the attribute at runtime; the prefers-color-scheme media query listener is only attached while theme === 'system'.

Warren brand mark wiring: src/ui/src/components/WarrenLogo.tsx renders the burrow-network mark as inline SVG using currentColor (inherits sidebar text color across themes); src/ui/public/favicon.svg is a self-contained variant with explicit grayscale fills + prefers-color-scheme style block — favicons cannot rely on currentColor across browsers. Vite auto-copies src/ui/public/* to dist/ root (no vite.config change). Geometry mirrors branding/generate-logo.py: pointed-top hex radius 32 around viewBox center 50,50; active node at index 1 (top-right).

src/ui/src/pages/RunDetail.tsx (pl-bb70 step 6 / warren-c845) renders a 'Seed' MetaCard adjacent to Burrow ID / Burrow Run when r.seedId !== null. Mirrors the Pull Request MetaCard's conditional render pattern; rendered as monospaced text only (no hyperlink yet) because warren has no seeds UI route — R-04 will land /#/seeds/:id and convert this into a clickable back-link without changing the data shape. src/ui/src/api/types.ts RunRow now carries seedId: string | null; CreateRunInput carries optional seedId?: string for completeness even though no UI form passes it yet.

agentsApi.list({projectId?}, signal?) (src/ui/src/api/client.ts, pl-fef5 step 8 / warren-f36c) takes a filter object first then an optional AbortSignal; empty projectId is converted to no query param (server rejects '?projectId='). agentsApi.get gained the same filter slot. agentsApi.refreshProject(projectId) hits POST /projects/:id/agents/refresh.

classifyAgentSource (src/ui/src/lib/agent-source.ts, pl-fef5 step 8 / warren-f36c) collapses AgentRow.source ('builtin' | 'library' | 'project:<id>' | undefined) into { tier, label, projectId }. Tier is the coarse classifier for badges; the full project id is preserved in projectId for tooltip/comparison.

Agents page (src/ui/src/pages/Agents.tsx, pl-fef5 step 8 / warren-f36c) keys row open-state on agentRowKey = '${source}::${name}' because the projectId filter can surface a global row and a project-tier row with the same name; a name-only key would expand both at once.

NewRun (src/ui/src/pages/NewRun.tsx, pl-fef5 step 8 / warren-f36c) scopes the agent picker to the selected project via useQuery(['agents', { projectId: project }], () => agentsApi.list(project ? { projectId: project } : {})); empty project falls back to a global-only fetch. AgentSourceHint renders a tier badge under the picker and flags project-tier rows whose projectId differs from the selected project.

title: Warren UI direction: thread/Plot-centric, not run-centric
rationale: Aligns warren with the network topology described in jayminwest.com/blog/12-agentic-networks. Run-centric UIs reify the orchestrator-to-worker pattern by making the human a dispatcher looking down at workers. Plot-centric UIs render the human as a peer node alongside agents. Tie-breaker test for any UI decision: does this make the human look like an operator above the agents, or a peer beside them? If 'above', it is hierarchy creeping back in and should be reconsidered. Steering becomes the default mode, dispatch the special case.

content: PlanRun UI surface (warren-a87f, pl-a258 step 7) lives at /plan-runs, /plan-runs/new, /plan-runs/:id. List page (src/ui/src/pages/PlanRuns.tsx) per-row fetches its own /plan-runs/:id detail to derive pending/in-flight/merged/failed child counts ('Np / Ni / Nm / Nf') because the list endpoint is row-only. Detail page (src/ui/src/pages/PlanRunDetail.tsx) polls every 5s and stops on PLAN_RUN_TERMINAL_STATES. NewPlanRun (src/ui/src/pages/NewPlanRun.tsx) reuses NewRun's defaults flow and disables every form field plus shows a 'Plan runs require .seeds/' banner when selectedProject.hasSeeds is false; planId is a free-text input, promptTemplate textarea defaults to 'work on sd {seed_id}'. Plot wiring is explicitly NOT surfaced here (warren-06dc owns Phase 2).

name: plan-run-state-badge
description: src/ui/src/components/PlanRunStateBadge.tsx exports PlanRunStateBadge (PlanRunState) and PlanRunChildStateBadge (PlanRunChildState) as siblings of the existing StateBadge for runs. Maps to the same Badge variants (queued/running/succeeded/failed/cancelled/secondary): child 'pending'->secondary, 'dispatched'+'pr_open'->queued, 'merged'->succeeded, 'skipped'->cancelled. Pattern: badges are state-enum-specific tiny modules rather than one overloaded StateBadge — keeps the type narrow and adding new state enums (preview, child, plan-run) cheap.

content: src/ui/src/api/client.ts streamPlanRunEvents and streamRunEvents share a private streamNdjsonEvents(basePath, opts) helper: both endpoints use the same server-side eventToNdjson serializer so the wire shape (RunEvent envelope, runId discriminator) matches. Route any new NDJSON tail endpoint through streamNdjsonEvents instead of copying the fetch+reader+JSON-parse loop.
